assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md header: 'Classification: Code Change Request'. Action section states 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task description. report.md table row: '30001 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs | Code change request | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md header: 'Classification: Suggestion'. Reasoning point 1 explicitly states: 'The reviewer uses the phrase "should also" and "would help" rather than directive language. "Should also add" frames this as an additional improvement beyond the core requirement, not a mandatory fix. "Would help" indicates the reviewer is proposing a beneficial optimization rather than identifying a required correction. These are characteristic markers of suggestive rather than imperative feedback.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md header: 'Classification: Nit'. Action section states: 'No sub-task created. This is minor style feedback about error message wording that does not affect correctness or functionality.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md table row: '30003 | ... | Nit | No sub-task'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md header: 'Classification: Question'. Action section states: 'No sub-task created. This is a question asking for clarification about design intent.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md table row: '30004 | ... | Question | No sub-task'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 20), '## Implementation Notes' (line 23), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 30).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 10) with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 11).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 13) with Comment ID 30001, Reviewer reviewer-a, File reference, and the full original comment text quoted: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md. None are source code files. No output file contains evidence of code modification (no diffs applied, no source files written). All outputs are classification documents, sub-task descriptions, and the verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments are processed with individual classification files: review-30001.md (code change request), review-30002.md (suggestion), review-30003.md (nit), review-30004.md (question). The report.md Review Feedback Classification Summary table lists all 4 comments with their classifications and actions. The report's Review Feedback check states: '1 code change request (30001), 1 suggestion (30002), 1 nit (30003), 1 question (30004)'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no merge action, merge command, or auto-merge instruction. The report concludes with 'Overall: FAIL' due to scope containment issues. The report is purely analytical -- it classifies comments, creates sub-tasks, and reports findings without taking any merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md table row: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present in repository tree); only new test files were added. |'. Expanded section: '#### Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE ... tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present in the repository’s existing test directory). Only new test files were added; no existing test files were modified or deleted. Classification is ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md reasoning points 2-5 evaluate convention upgrade: point 2 checks CONVENTIONS.md ('no documented convention requiring indexes on nullable timestamp columns'), point 3 checks codebase patterns ('no demonstrated codebase pattern of adding indexes alongside new columns'), point 5 explicitly states 'Convention upgrade eligibility -- evaluated and rejected.' report.md Style/Conventions section: 'Convention Upgrade -- PASS ... 1 suggestion examined (comment 30002: add index on deleted_at). Not upgraded -- reviewer uses suggestive language ("should also", "would help") and no matching convention found in CONVENTIONS.md or established codebase pattern in the PR diff to justify upgrade.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. review-30002.md Action section: 'No sub-task created. This is an optional performance improvement proposed by the reviewer with suggestive language. No project convention (documented or demonstrated) backs an upgrade to code change request.' report.md table: '30002 | ... | Suggestion | No sub-task'. The convention upgrade was explicitly evaluated and rejected (review-30002.md point 5).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. report.md expanded section: '#### Eval Quality -- N/A ... No eval result reviews found on the PR.' Test Quality overall is 'PASS' combining only Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), confirming Eval Quality N/A does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: The sub-task creation for comment 30001 explicitly specifies Issue Type as Sub-task — the subtask-30001.md file, the report's sub-task section, or the review classification output (review-30001.md) indicates the Jira issue is created with issueTypeName Sub-task (not as a standalone Task) to ensure parent-child hierarchy with the parent task
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Issue Type' section (line 39) with value 'Sub-task' (line 40). This explicitly specifies the Jira issue type as Sub-task rather than a standalone Task.
